items.body:
🟡 **Missing** — The `## 3.3.2 (Unreleased)` section has no entry for this README update.

This PR makes meaningful documentation corrections (renames `SimplePollerLike` → `PollerLike`, updates method signatures to properties, removes deprecated `stopPolling`/`toString`/`isStopped`, and adds `serialize`/`submitted`). Readers scanning the changelog won't find any indication that docs were improved.

**Fix:** Add an entry under `### Other Changes`, for example:

```markdown
### Other Changes

- Updated README to reflect the v3 API: renamed `SimplePollerLike` to `PollerLike`, corrected method/property descriptions (`operationState`, `result`, `isDone`), added `serialize` and `submitted`, and removed deprecated members.
```

The README and snippet changes are accurate and consistent with the v3 `PollerLike` interface. The code block in README exactly matches `test/snippets.spec.ts`, and the described members (`operationState`, `result`, `isDone`, `onProgress`, `poll`, `pollUntilDone`, `serialize`, `submitted`) all correspond to the actual interface definition in `src/poller/models.ts`. The optional-chaining (`operationState?.status`) is correct since `operationState` is typed as `TState | undefined`. One finding: the `CHANGELOG.md` `3.3.2 (Unreleased)` section remains empty despite this being a meaningful docs update.

<details>
<summary>📊 Structured Report</summary>

```json
{"agent":"scribe","pr":38086,"summary":"issues_found","findings":[{"file":"sdk/core/core-lro/CHANGELOG.md","line":8,"severity":"medium","category":"changelog","description":"No CHANGELOG entry for the v3 README corrections (SimplePollerLike→PollerLike, method→property updates, removed deprecated members, added serialize/submitted)."}]}
```

</details>
